name: peartune-host
description: The PearTune host: an always-on daemon that serves a self-hosted music library over HyperDHT.
//: THE HOST HAS ITS OWN MANIFEST, and this is not tidiness.
The repo root's package.json is the PHONE APP: React Native, Expo, expo-audio,
jsQR, Phosphor. The host is a Node server that shares only protocol/ and
client/ with it. Building the image from the root manifest meant:
1. It DID NOT BUILD AT ALL. The root has `postinstall: patch-package` (for the
app's expo-audio patch), and patch-package is a devDependency - so
`npm ci --omit=dev` installed no patch-package and then tried to run it.
Exit 127, every time. `--ignore-scripts` is NOT the way out: it also skips
the native postinstalls the hypercore stack needs, and you get a runtime
that half-works and then aborts (see the suite-wide note about bare deps).
2. It dragged React Native and Expo into a server image that has no use for
These eleven are what host/, client/ and protocol/ actually require. Keep them
in step with the root manifest by hand: they are the SAME wire, and a version
skew between the phone and the host is a protocol bug waiting to happen.
